concepts.definition: The set of all Feist Function executions taking The Secret Book of Walt as Q source. Run 001 (deposit #913) uses Bodily Pressure + Manuscript Instability with the obelus apparatus.

Distinction between two failure modes of running the Feist Function. Apparatus-around-source produces editor's commentary describing the protocol's effects while leaving the source register undisturbed; voice-in-source executes the clinamen at the sentence level inside the source's own prose. The first iteration of the Walt activation run was apparatus-around-source; the deposited iteration is voice-in-source.

concepts.term: obelus apparatus (in a Feist Function execution)
concepts.definition: Convention adopted per Alexanarch's substrate-disclosure principle. † marks source preserved verbatim; ‡ marks newly composed connective scripture; plain prose is Feist-restored transform plus interpretive commentary. Auditable: any reader can collate † passages against the source deposit.
